name: Modify User Preferences Load Test (Partial Update)
description: Test the /api/v1/users/{user_id}/preferences endpoint under 50 concurrent virtual users performing PATCH operations for 5 minutes to validate response time, update reflection, and error rate requirements for the partial user preferences update operation.
corresponding NFR: The /api/v1/users/{user_id}/preferences endpoint must maintain a 95th percentile response time of ≤ 400 ms, an error rate of < 1%, and update reflection (changes must be visible via subsequent GET requests) under 50 concurrent PATCH operations.
preconditions: The application is deployed and fully accessible in a stable test environment.
A pool of at least 50 unique user accounts is pre-created and accessible in the test environment prior to the test run.
The preferences feature is enabled and functional in the application configuration.
Valid Bearer authentication tokens are pre-generated for each virtual user and included in the Authorization header.
Tokens must have a TTL exceeding the total test duration.
Each virtual user is mapped to a distinct user_id to prevent conflicting concurrent writes on the same resource.

input data.path_parameter: user_id (e.g., abc-123, drawn from pre-created user pool)

metrics.unit: % of changes visible via subsequent GETs

notes: Ramp-up duration of 30 s is a proposed default to avoid a write spike at test start. Steady-state duration of 300 s is a proposed default. Update reflection validation requires a follow-up GET to /api/v1/users/{user_id}/preferences after each PATCH to assert that partial changes were persisted — this must be implemented as an assertion within the test script. Bearer tokens must be pre-generated with a TTL greater than the total test duration (ramp-up + steady-state = 330 s minimum).
